title: Aaron Levie: Another new idea to push the state of AI architectures forward. Sakana released a model that effectively uses...
summary: Follow Builders 记录到 Aaron Levie（@levie） 在 X 上的最新动态。简介：ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI。要点：1. Another new idea to push the state of AI architectures forward. Sakana released a model that effectively uses a mixture of models to get work done. You get a single API but then t... 2. Agents will use software 100X more than people. When that happens, theres a huge need for guardrails on what the agents are doing so they don’t leak data or change the wrong infor...
raw_text:
{"builder": {"source": "x", "name": "Aaron Levie", "handle": "levie", "bio": "ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI", "tweets": [{"id": "2068917230570795178", "text": "Another new idea to push the state of AI architectures forward. Sakana released a model that effectively uses a mixture of models to get work done. You get a single API but then the work gets farmed out the model that best performs the task. 

“Fugu manages model selection, delegation, verification, and synthesis automatically. It solves tasks directly when that is enough, or coordinates a team of expert models when a problem calls for more. The complexity of a multi-agent system never reaches your code.”

This is generally how applied AI products are building their agent harnesses at this point, but the idea of making this an LLM that any developer can interact with is also a great idea. As we get more innovation with both frontier closed and OSS models, there’s going to be a ton of value produced for the layer that can route the best.", "createdAt": "2026-06-22T04:41:21.000Z", "url": "https://x.com/levie/status/2068917230570795178", "likes": 206, "retweets": 23, "replies": 25, "isQuote": true, "quotedTweetId": "2068861630327443966"}, {"id": "2068851573175021864", "text": "Agents will use software 100X more than people. 

When that happens, theres a huge need for guardrails on what the agents are doing so they don’t leak data or change the wrong information, authoritative sources of truth for them to work with, logging and auditing of what they’re doing, the ability to collaborate with people through these systems, and more.

A simple query on any given agentic task could pull in more data than a user touches in a month. As a result, there are lots of categories of software that when it goes headless that the usage and value go up substantially. Agents will end up using our CRM data, documents and corporate knowledge, analytics data, and other information far more than people ever did.

The platforms that can move toward the model of powering these headless interactions, and have a business model and technology strategy to support this, will be in the best position in the future.", "createdAt": "2026-06-22T00:20:27.000Z", "url": "https://x.com/levie/status/2068851573175021864", "likes": 228, "retweets": 44, "replies": 34, "isQuote": true, "quotedTweetId": "2068431946431008972"}]}, "tweets": [{"id": "2068917230570795178", "text": "Another new idea to push the state of AI architectures forward. Sakana released a model that effectively uses a mixture of models to get work done. You get a single API but then the work gets farmed out the model that best performs the task. 

“Fugu manages model selection, delegation, verification, and synthesis automatically. It solves tasks directly when that is enough, or coordinates a team of expert models when a problem calls for more. The complexity of a multi-agent system never reaches your code.”

This is ge...

raw_text:
{"builder": {"source": "x", "name": "Zara Zhang", "handle": "zarazhangrui", "bio": "Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w", "tweets": [{"id": "2068964055235321954", "text": "I’m talking about context, not prompt", "createdAt": "2026-06-22T07:47:24.000Z", "url": "https://x.com/zarazhangrui/status/2068964055235321954", "likes": 1, "retweets": 0, "replies": 0, "isQuote": false, "quotedTweetId": null}, {"id": "2068923768500793603", "text": "A good rule of thumb for preventing AI slop (in writing, design, etc):

Is your input (the context) longer than the output? 

I've found that for the AI to produce quality results, my input is often 3-5 times the length of the output.

If your input is much shorter than the output, it's almost certainly going to produce slop", "createdAt": "2026-06-22T05:07:19.000Z", "url": "https://x.com/zarazhangrui/status/2068923768500793603", "likes": 131, "retweets": 4, "replies": 32, "isQuote": false, "quotedTweetId": null}]}, "tweets": [{"id": "2068964055235321954", "text": "I’m talking about context, not prompt", "createdAt": "2026-06-22T07:47:24.000Z", "url": "https://x.com/zarazhangrui/status/2068964055235321954", "likes": 1, "retweets": 0, "replies": 0, "isQuote": false, "quotedTweetId": null}, {"id": "2068923768500793603", "text": "A good rule of thumb for preventing AI slop (in writing, design, etc):
